title: Isoetes phrygia
taxonomy: Isoetes phrygia is a species of quillwort that belongs to the family Isoetaceae. This plant is classified as a type of vascular plant, specifically within the division Lycopodiophyta. The kingdom it belongs to is Plantae, and the phylum is Lycopodiophyta. Isoetes phrygia is further classified into the class Lycopodiopsida and the order Selaginellales. It is also known by other names such as Phrygian quillwort and quillwort.

Isoetes phrygia is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. intro: Isoetes phrygia, also known as Phrygian quillwort, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the genus Isoetes, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and unique, quill-like appearance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Isoetes phrygia is rich in bioactive constituents such as quassinoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
